scope: Read-only audit of the current combined tree after Cycle 2 Round 1 remediation, QA, and performance changes.

findings.title: Stop & clean does not stop either running drill
findings.evidence: Shark cleanup never clears a run flag; Red Team clears _running but neither unconditional jitter nor phase/step loops consult it. Both workers continue after the UI reports a stop request.
findings.exploitability: No attacker required; operator stopping a live multi-phase drill reliably reaches the faulty lifecycle path.
findings.impact: Later stages can write new markers, spawn tagged processes, make the Shark test connection, sustain CPU/UI load, and race cleanup after Stop & clean.
findings.remediation: Use a dedicated cancellation event, interruptible waits, cancellation checks at every phase/stage boundary, bounded non-GUI join, and final cleanup; test stop-during-jitter with zero later side effects.

findings.title: Emergency shutdown ownership matching still reaches unrelated Python processes
findings.evidence: StartsWith(root) lacks a directory boundary, and any command line containing the root substring is treated as Angerona-owned.
findings.exploitability: Requires an operator to run the elevated recovery batch, plus a sibling-prefix interpreter or unrelated Python job with an Angerona path argument.
findings.impact: Can terminate unrelated notebooks, automation, tools, or sibling installations; the image-wide Ollama kill remains removed.
findings.remediation: Prefer PID/start-time/executable identity; otherwise require the exact suite interpreter and a parsed entry point under the root with boundary-aware canonical comparison. Do not infer ownership from an arbitrary argument substring.

findings.title: ARIA confirmation can execute a different callback than the operator previewed
findings.evidence: Pending confirmations bind only tool name, mutable arguments, and time; confirm resolves the current mutable registry entry and executes its current callback without checking original identity, kind, preview, or digest.
findings.exploitability: A component with access to a future shared Assistant can replace a registered name or mutate nested staged arguments before the operator confirms.
findings.impact: A valid token can authorize an action different from its preview once ARIA tools are dynamically integrated.
findings.remediation: Bind an immutable staged WRITE action to tool version/callable identity, canonical arguments, preview, expiry, and digest; invalidate tokens on registry change and test replacement/mutation/replay paths.

findings.title: ARIA default research READ performs browser and egress side effects without confirmation
findings.evidence: The research tool defaults open_in_browser to true, is registered as READ, and immediately opens all allow-listed source URLs with the indicator embedded.
findings.exploitability: Once wired, any caller/prompt/voice input that invokes research can trigger the side effect; destinations are limited to the source allow-list.
findings.impact: Unexpected browser tabs and disclosure of an internal hash, IP, domain, URL, or CVE to third-party sites without per-action confirmation.
findings.remediation: Keep URL construction as pure READ; make external opening an explicit WRITE/EGRESS action with destination/indicator preview and confirmation, default opening false, and test zero opener calls on READ.

verified_without_new_regression: AAR exact path and PID-plus-token correlation with bounded windows
D-drive production and direct-selfcheck environment setup
graceful model-specific Ollama unload without image-wide runner kill
